domain_experience: 6 years building internal tools for HVAC distributors
understanding HVAC quoting, ordering, and back-office workflows
exposure to operational pain points in the HVAC ecosystem
assets: ability to build custom software and automations
direct access to a small HVAC service business through brother
ability to test workflows in a real operating environment
distribution: warm introductions through brother to regional HVAC business owners
network_access: brother runs a small HVAC service business
brother knows multiple local HVAC owners
constraints: should avoid long speculative product build before validation
credibility_signals: technical ability to ship the stack personally
access to real operators for rapid feedback
unfair_advantages: better HVAC workflow understanding than generic AI consultants
clear service-to-software path inside one vertical

opportunities.title: Revenue recovery automation service for HVAC shops
opportunities.thesis: Sell a done-for-you service that recovers lost revenue from missed calls, unbooked estimates, and stale maintenance follow-ups using AI-assisted messaging, summaries, and automation.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user understands HVAC workflow friction, can translate technical fixes into owner outcomes, and has warm access to likely buyers through his brother.
validation_plan: Interview owners about missed calls, stale estimates, and renewal follow-up
Pitch a fixed-scope paid pilot tied to recovered bookings or reopened estimates
Run the workflow manually with lightweight tools before deeper integrations
tool_specs: AI-generated call summaries and follow-up drafts
Human approval workflow for outbound messages
kill_criteria: Owners will discuss the pain but will not pay for a pilot
Each account requires fully custom integrations that destroy margins

opportunities.title: Dispatcher and CSR workflow audit plus implementation
opportunities.thesis: Offer a paid operational audit for inbound call handling, estimate follow-up, and service agreement renewals, then implement targeted automations.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can diagnose HVAC-specific process leaks faster than a generic automation freelancer and can implement fixes directly.
validation_plan: Pitch a paid audit to 3-5 owners
Use a simple scorecard for call handling, estimate follow-up, and membership renewals
Convert audit findings into a fixed implementation offer
tool_specs: Basic automation recipes for common HVAC follow-up tasks
kill_criteria: Audits generate interest but not implementation work
Owners see the audit as consulting fluff rather than operational value

opportunities.title: HVAC estimate follow-up and close-rate micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow tool that tracks open estimates, drafts follow-ups, flags stalled deals, and gives owners simple visibility into quote conversion.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can shape the product around real HVAC quoting behavior and common edge cases learned from pilots.
validation_plan: Prove that estimate follow-up is the most acute repeated pain across pilots
Test whether owners will pay for that workflow separate from broader service
Prototype with manual or semi-manual delivery before self-serve software
kill_criteria: The workflow is too entangled with each shop's CRM to standardize
Owners prefer managed service over software even after proof of ROI

opportunities.title: HVAC operating benchmark and workflow playbook subscription
opportunities.thesis: Aggregate anonymized operating patterns from service shops and sell benchmarks, templates, and recurring workflow playbooks.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user could eventually build proprietary insight from client work, but only after earning enough trust and collecting enough repeated data.
validation_plan: Confirm owners want comparative insight, not just execution help
Gather repeatable metrics from early clients
Test willingness to pay for recurring benchmarks or playbooks
kill_criteria: Not enough shops will share data
Benchmarks are interesting but not budget-worthy

opportunities.title: HVAC revenue recovery concierge to automation layer to micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start by manually delivering revenue recovery for a few HVAC shops, productize the repeated parts into an internal operator console, then spin that into a narrow SaaS for estimate follow-up, missed-call recovery, and renewal workflows.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has the rare combination of HVAC workflow fluency, engineering ability, and warm access to early buyers, which creates a strong service-first wedge and a credible later product moat.
validation_plan: Use brother's network to secure 5-8 owner conversations in the first two weeks
Close one paid pilot for revenue recovery using a low-friction offer
Track which workflow repeats most consistently across shops
Productize only the repeated workflow after a pilot proves ROI
tool_specs: Concierge operations board for leads, estimates, and follow-up status
AI-generated call summaries and follow-up suggestions
Template library for missed-call text-back, estimate nudges, and renewal follow-up
Outcome dashboard showing recovered jobs and reopened pipeline
kill_criteria: Fewer than 5 meaningful owner conversations from the warm network within 2 weeks
No owner is willing to pay for a pilot tied to a clear revenue problem
No single workflow repeats enough to standardize into software

next_actions: Get 8-12 warm intro targets from brother
Run 5 owner interviews focused on missed calls, stale estimates, and renewals
Pitch one paid pilot for a revenue recovery service
Use the pilot to identify the first narrow SaaS wedge
Avoid building broad contractor AI software before a repeated workflow proves out
